name: Observable Plot (Exploratory Data Visualization)

auth.notes: Client-side library; Observable notebooks require account for cloud features

performance.notes: SVG rendering; fast for typical datasets; Canvas marks for large datasets

agent_readiness.notes: Modern successor to D3 from Mike Bostock. Grammar of Graphics approach: Plot.plot({marks: [Plot.dot(data, {x, y}), Plot.line(...)]}). Marks: dot, line, bar, area, text, tick, rect, ruleX/Y, geo. Scales auto-inferred. Faceting, groupBy, bin, normalize transforms. Much easier than D3 for exploratory analysis. Works in Observable notebooks, Node.js, React. Includes Voronoi, hexbin, density. svelte-plot and react-plot wrappers. Designed for exploratory analysis.

gotchas: Smaller ecosystem than Plotly or Vega-Lite
Not all chart types available (no treemaps, sunburst yet)
best_when: Exploratory data analysis in browser or Observable notebooks
Teams wanting D3 power with much simpler API
avoid_when: Complex interactive dashboards (use Vega-Altair or Plotly)
Highly custom visualizations requiring D3 low-level control
summary: Observable Plot is Mike Bostock's modern successor to D3 for exploratory data visualization. Grammar of Graphics approach with Marks (dot, line, bar, area, text). Auto-inferred scales, faceting, and statistical transforms. Much simpler than D3 while still flexible. Works in Observable notebooks, Node.js, and React. Best for JavaScript data analysis and exploratory visualization without D3's verbosity.
